title: 无标题文章
summary: Everyone says they're building an AI-native company. But what does that mean? Automating what you're already doing with AI is not enough. content:
Everyone says they're building an AI-native company. But what does that mean?

Automating what you're already doing with AI is not enough. A truly AI-native company rethinks every role and tears down the walls between them.

- QA doesn't just file bugs: they point agents at them and file the fixes too
- PMs don't just ship a 70%-done PRD and wait for eng to hit the edge cases: they point agents to walk the real code, surface those cases, and flush them out with test cases
- - Designers don't just land up with Figma mocks: they prototype on real Android 12 devices 😜 to test how those animations and UX actually hold up
- Data scientists don't just wait for real data: they run simulations up front, generate synthetic data, and build intuition around which A/B option wins

So what's left for engineers? Plenty.

I wrote a sketch of what that might look like, in detail, here: https://kau.sh/blog/ai-native-company/
